text.content:
Weapon Materials

Athas is a metal-poor world. Weapons are priced and sold in the coin type of their material. On a natural 1 attack roll, roll 1d6 and consult the table below.

MaterialCoinsBreaks on 1d6
WoodBitsAutomatically breaks
StoneCP1–4
BoneBP1–3
ObsidianSP1–2
MetalGP1 (Steel never breaks)
MagicalGPNever breaks

Armor Materials

When a natural 20 hits you, roll 1d6 and consult the table below.

MaterialArmor TypeCoinsBreaks on 1d6
LeatherLeather, ShieldBits1–4
HideLeatherCP1–3
BoneChainmail equivalentBP1–3
ChitinChainmail, Plate, ShieldSP1–2
MetalPlate, ShieldGP1
MagicalAnyGPNever breaks
Metal Armor: Wearing metal armor causes water needs and consumption to double, and imposes penalties from dehydration. Metal shields do not apply this penalty.

Masterwork Weapons & Armor

A masterwork weapon or armor grants +1 to all attack rolls and +1 to damage. Masterwork items cost double their base price. Masterwork and magical properties can stack.

Repairing Equipment

Broken weapons and armor can be repaired for 50% of their original cost, rounded up.

Coin Types on Athas

  • Bits — Fractional ceramic pieces; the cheapest currency
  • CP — Ceramic pieces; the standard Athasian coin
  • BP — Bronze pieces; mid-tier currency
  • SP — Silver pieces; valuable
  • GP — Gold pieces; rare and precious

Characters start with 3d6 × 10 CP.
